strengths: 18+ years of software experience with recent full-stack TypeScript/Node.js, React, MongoDB and AWS work at Optilyz and re:cinq
Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure experience, including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling, matching the AI agent development requirement
Strong engineering-craft signals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing workshops, and open-source maintenance
concerns: No evidence of Nest.js, and no Vitest, Jest or Playwright anywhere on the resume — testing expertise is concentrated in self-authored D-language tools (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) rather than the role's primary stack
Required multi-language proficiency is thin: no Kotlin or Python at all, and Java exposure is dated (2016–2018, robotics context), undermining the secondary Kotlin/Java backend responsibilities

Frontend depth skews heavily to Ember.js across multiple roles and side projects; React is named in only one position, and years of investment in the niche DLang ecosystem suggest distance from the mainstream TypeScript toolchain this role centers on

key_factors.factor: Missing required languages (Kotlin, Python; stale Java)

response.justification: While the candidate clears the experience bar and has genuine recent TypeScript/Node.js and AI-agent work, the fit against this specific stack is weaker than the resume's length suggests. The role's core toolchain — Nest.js, Vitest, Playwright — is entirely absent; his documented testing depth lives in the D-language ecosystem, and his frontend history is dominated by Ember.js rather than React. Kotlin and Python, both listed as required, are missing, and Java is several years stale. Much of the resume's seniority rests on niche technologies (DLang, Ember, custom CMSs) with limited transfer to this team's standardized stack, so meaningful ramp-up risk exists on precisely the technologies the team uses daily.
